Reciprocity
Most Common Mistakes
● Remember that gifts are unconditional. Just because
someone says that a free oil change is a gift if you get
a set of new tires, that doesn’t make that oil change a
gift. If you’re told you’ll be given the gift of a free trip
to Disneyland if you reach 25% over your sales goals,
that doesn’t make the Disneyland trip a gift.
● Giving “gifts” with logos on it from the company.
That’s not reciprocity, that’s advertising.
● Misunderstanding the difference between rewards
and gifts – for example a water cooler in the waiting
room. This isn’t a gift if people need to help
themselves. It becomes an entitlement.
● Misunderstanding the golden rule to be about paying
people back instead of proactively giving in order to
activate reciprocity.
● Thinking that gifts have to be tangible.
● Mistaking rewards for gifts.
○ Remember, gifts are proactive and come before
the desired behavior while rewards come after.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 1 --
